summary: Linked the common fighter FallSpecial translation unit by replacing a duplicated/nonmatching workaround in ftCo_FallSpecial.c with a shared static inline helper and marking the object Matching in configure.py. The PR had no body and no human review comments; the only comment was the decomp.dev GALE01 report showing linked code increased by 1236 bytes (+0.03%).

key_files.role: Reworked FallSpecial functions to match, including ftCo_80096900 and ftCo_800969D8 sharing a static inline implementation.

what_changed: configure.py now registers ftCo_FallSpecial.c as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Removed the previous SOLUTION macro and todo comment around ftCo_80096900.
Introduced static inline void inline0(...) to share the FallSpecial setup logic between ftCo_80096900 and ftCo_800969D8.
ftCo_80096900 now calls inline0 with arg6 set to 0 and the final boolean set to true.
ftCo_800969D8 now uses PAD_STACK(8) and calls inline0 with the supplied arg6 and the final boolean set to false.

The shared helper parameterizes the ChangeMotionState start-frame/blend argument through arg6 and conditionally calls ftCommon_8007D698 only when the new boolean parameter is true and the fighter is airborne.
Several direct gobj->user_data Fighter accesses were replaced with GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
The previous local stack dummy array in ftCo_800969D8 was replaced with PAD_STACK(8); the stack dummy in ftCo_80096CC8 was removed in the excerpt.
smart_moves: The matching fix avoided maintaining two nearly identical hand-expanded bodies by extracting their common sequence into a static inline helper, while exposing the small behavioral/codegen differences as parameters.
Using a boolean to distinguish the airborne ftCommon_8007D698 path preserved the observed difference between ftCo_80096900 and ftCo_800969D8 without duplicating the whole routine.
Replacing the zeroed byte-array stack placeholder with PAD_STACK(8) made the stack-shaping intent explicit.
Switching to GET_FIGHTER(gobj) aligns the file with common fighter accessor style and may help produce expected codegen versus direct user_data access.
decomp_lessons: When two functions differ only by a small argument or conditional side effect, a static inline helper can be a better matching tactic than macro-selected duplicate implementations.
For stack padding needed only for matching, prefer explicit PAD_STACK usage over anonymous byte arrays when the project convention supports it.
After a source file is confirmed matching, update configure.py immediately from NonMatching to Matching so linked-code metrics and build status reflect the progress.
Small accessor changes such as gobj->user_data to GET_FIGHTER(gobj) can matter for consistency and possibly for matching in fighter code.
naming_conventions: Common fighter file and symbols use the ftCo_ prefix, as in ftCo_FallSpecial.c, ftCo_80096900, ftCo_800969D8, ftCo_FallSpecial_Anim, and ftCo_FallSpecial_Coll.
Motion-state and state-machine names preserve mixed semantic/hashed style: ftCo_MS_FallSpecial, ftCo_SM_FallSpecial, ftCo_SM_FallSpecialF, ftCo_SM_FallSpecialB.
The temporary shared helper was named inline0, indicating a codegen/matching helper rather than a fully understood semantic routine.
Fighter data access convention in this diff favors GET_FIGHTER(gobj) over direct gobj->user_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Static inline helper used to force shared inlined code shape across ftCo_80096900 and ftCo_800969D8.
Parameterized arg6 into Fighter_ChangeMotionState so one wrapper passes 0 while the other passes its float argument.
Parameterized an extra boolean controlling the airborne ftCommon_8007D698 call, matching the apparent distinction between the two entry points.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in ftCo_800969D8 to preserve stack layout.
Removed the prior compile-time SOLUTION selector once the inline-based match was found.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed linked code at 27.92%, an increase of +0.03% / +1236 bytes, with 'No changes' otherwise.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other ftCommon files with duplicated wrapper bodies that can be collapsed into static inline helpers for matching.
Search for remaining gobj->user_data accesses in fighter code that should use GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Search for anonymous u8 stack padding arrays that can be replaced with PAD_STACK.
Review other FallSpecial-related functions for semantic names that could eventually replace inline0 and unknown movement fields.
